name: Swedish Limpa Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure the warm water is between 105°F and 115°F.
2. Pour the warm water into a large bowl.
3. Sprinkle the yeast over the water and stir until dissolved.
4. Add the salt, molasses, brown sugar, first measure of melted butter, orange peel, anise seed, and rye flour to the bowl. Mix well.
5. Gradually add the all-purpose flour by hand until the dough is stiff enough to lift from the side of the bowl.
6. Turn out onto a lightly floured pastry cloth or board (dust with all-purpose flour).
7. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
8. Place in a lightly greased bowl. Cover with a towel.
9. Let rise in a warm (85°F) place, free from drafts, until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 hours.
10. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
11. Grease a large cookie sheet and sprinkle lightly with cornmeal.
12. Punch down the dough.
13. Turn out onto a lightly floured pastry cloth or board.
14. Divide into as many loaves as desired.
15. Shape each into a smooth ball about 6 inches in diameter, tuck the edges under.
16. Place on the cookie sheet with lots of room between.
17. Form into ovals, 8 inches long with tapering ends.
18. Make 3 diagonal slashes on the top of each loaf, about 1/4 inch deep.
19. Cover with a towel and let rise in a warm, draft-free location until doubled in bulk, 1-1 1/2 hours.
20. Bake on the middle shelf for 30 to 35 minutes.
21. Cover loosely with aluminum foil during the last 10 minutes of baking.
22. Remove to a rack. Brush with melted butter.
23. Serve slightly warm.
